sleep.text: Sleep is the best meditation.
sleep.author: Dalai Lama

sleep.text: Calm mind brings inner strength and self-confidence.
sleep.author: Dalai Lama

sleep.text: Do not let the behavior of others destroy your inner peace.
sleep.author: Dalai Lama

sleep.text: Silence is a source of great strength.
sleep.author: Lao Tzu

sleep.text: Be content with what you have; rejoice in the way things are.
sleep.author: Lao Tzu

sleep.text: Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished.
sleep.author: Lao Tzu

sleep.text: When I let go of what I am, I become what I might be.
sleep.author: Lao Tzu

sleep.text: He who is contented is rich.
sleep.author: Lao Tzu

sleep.text: Listen to silence. It has so much to say.
sleep.author: Rumi

sleep.text: Out beyond ideas of wrongdoing and rightdoing, there is a field. I'll meet you there.
sleep.author: Rumi

sleep.text: When you do things from your soul, you feel a river moving in you, a joy.
sleep.author: Rumi

sleep.text: The wound is the place where the light enters you.
sleep.author: Rumi

sleep.text: Sleep is the golden chain that ties health and our bodies together.
sleep.author: Thomas Dekker

sleep.text: Smile, breathe, and go slowly.
sleep.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

sleep.text: The breath is the bridge between body and mind. Cross it gently.
sleep.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

sleep.text: Peace is every step.
sleep.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

sleep.text: Letting go gives us freedom, and freedom is the only condition for happiness.
sleep.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

sleep.text: Within yourself is a stillness to which you can retreat at any time.
sleep.author: Hermann Hesse

sleep.text: There is more to life than increasing its speed.
sleep.author: Mahatma Gandhi

sleep.text: Each night, when I go to sleep, I die. The next morning, I am reborn.
sleep.author: Mahatma Gandhi

sleep.text: He who lives in harmony with himself lives in harmony with the universe.
sleep.author: Marcus Aurelius

sleep.text: Dwell on the beauty of life. Watch the stars, and see yourself running with them.
sleep.author: Marcus Aurelius

sleep.text: Very little is needed to make a happy life. It is all within yourself.
sleep.author: Marcus Aurelius

sleep.text: Rest when you're weary. Refresh and renew yourself. Then get back to work.
sleep.author: Ralph Marston

sleep.text: Almost everything will work again if you unplug it for a few minutes, including you.
sleep.author: Anne Lamott

sleep.text: The cure for anything is salt water: sweat, tears, or the sea.
sleep.author: Isak Dinesen

sleep.text: Set peace of mind as your highest goal, and organize your life around it.
sleep.author: Brian Tracy

sleep.text: Quiet the mind, and the soul will speak.
sleep.author: Ma Jaya Sati Bhagavati

sleep.text: Tension is who you think you should be. Relaxation is who you are.
sleep.author: Chinese proverb

sleep.text: Sleep is the best cure for waking troubles.
sleep.author: Miguel de Cervantes

sleep.text: A well-spent day brings happy sleep.
sleep.author: Leonardo da Vinci

sleep.text: The night is the mother of counsel.
sleep.author: Greek proverb

sleep.text: When the mind is calm, how smoothly you perceive everything.
sleep.author: Paramahansa Yogananda

sleep.text: Stillness is the altar of spirit.
sleep.author: Paramahansa Yogananda

sleep.text: The quieter you become, the more you can hear.
sleep.author: Ram Dass

sleep.text: Empty your mind. Be formless, shapeless, like water.
sleep.author: Bruce Lee

sleep.text: Sleep, that knits up the raveled sleeve of care.
sleep.author: William Shakespeare

sleep.text: We are such stuff as dreams are made on, and our little life is rounded with a sleep.
sleep.author: William Shakespeare

sleep.text: The earth has music for those who listen.
sleep.author: William Shakespeare

sleep.text: The mind in its own place can make a heaven of hell, a hell of heaven.
sleep.author: John Milton

sleep.text: Let the soft animal of your body love what it loves.
sleep.author: Mary Oliver

sleep.text: Wherever you are, be there totally.
sleep.author: Eckhart Tolle

sleep.text: A quiet conscience makes one strong.
sleep.author: Anne Frank

sleep.text: The mind is everything. What you think you become.
sleep.author: Buddha

sleep.text: Peace comes from within. Do not seek it without.
sleep.author: Buddha

sleep.text: Better than a thousand hollow words is one word that brings peace.
sleep.author: Buddha

sleep.text: Let go or be dragged.
sleep.author: Zen proverb

sleep.text: Tomorrow's sun will rise whether you worry or not.
sleep.author: African proverb

sleep.text: Tomorrow is a new day; begin it with too high a spirit to be encumbered by old nonsense.
sleep.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

sleep.text: Adopt the pace of nature: her secret is patience.
sleep.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

sleep.text: The mind that is anxious about future events is miserable.
sleep.author: Seneca

sleep.text: We do not remember days, we remember moments.
sleep.author: Cesare Pavese

sleep.text: The greatest weapon against stress is our ability to choose one thought over another.
sleep.author: William James

sleep.text: There will come a time when you believe everything is finished; that will be the beginning.
sleep.author: Louis L'Amour

sleep.text: Slow down. Calm down. Don't worry. Don't hurry. Trust the process.
sleep.author: Alexandra Stoddard

sleep.text: Silence is the sleep that nourishes wisdom.
sleep.author: Francis Bacon

sleep.text: After silence, that which comes nearest to expressing the inexpressible is music.
sleep.author: Aldous Huxley

sleep.text: Let everything happen to you: beauty and terror. Just keep going. No feeling is final.
sleep.author: Rainer Maria Rilke

sleep.text: Live the questions now. Perhaps you will live, some distant day, into the answers.
sleep.author: Rainer Maria Rilke

sleep.text: Worry is the misuse of imagination.
sleep.author: Dan Zadra

sleep.text: A ruffled mind makes a restless pillow.
sleep.author: Charlotte Bronte

sleep.text: Night is the other half of life, and the better half.
sleep.author: Johann Wolfgang von Goethe

sleep.text: There is a time for many words, and there is also a time for sleep.
sleep.author: Homer

sleep.text: Take rest; a field that has rested gives a bountiful crop.
sleep.author: Ovid

sleep.text: The best bridge between despair and hope is a good night's sleep.
sleep.author: E. Joseph Cossman

sleep.text: Even a soul submerged in sleep is hard at work and helps make something of the world.
sleep.author: Heraclitus

sleep.text: And into the forest I go, to lose my mind and find my soul.
sleep.author: John Muir

sleep.text: Patience is the companion of wisdom.
sleep.author: Saint Augustine

sleep.text: The greatest wealth is to live content with little.
sleep.author: Plato

sleep.text: It's not stress that kills us, it is our reaction to it.
sleep.author: Hans Selye

sleep.text: What the caterpillar calls the end of the world, the master calls a butterfly.
sleep.author: Richard Bach

sleep.text: I am still learning.
sleep.author: Michelangelo

sleep.text: Solitude is where I place my chaos to rest and awaken my inner peace.
sleep.author: Nikki Rowe

sleep.text: Quiet people have the loudest minds.
sleep.author: Stephen Hawking

sleep.text: The night, in some ways, holds the day together.
sleep.author: Wendell Berry

sleep.text: Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom.
sleep.author: Aristotle

sleep.text: The night is not your enemy. It is the rest you have earned.
Let go of what was. Let go of what might be. Let go.
Now I lay me down. The day is finished. The work is enough.
There is a quiet inside you that doesn't need anything.
The body knows how to sleep. Stop teaching it.
Surrender to the night. It is wiser than the day.
Tomorrow will ask its own questions. Tonight, only this breath.
Tonight, be the kindest version of yourself to yourself.
The body sinks. The mind softens. The day releases its grip.
The trees release their leaves without grief. You may release the day.
Forgive yourself for what you did not finish. The list will keep.
Sleep is not absence; it is presence with yourself.
Even the river rests, somewhere, far upstream.
The moon does not ask permission to rise. Sleep asks nothing of you.
Within you is a sanctuary. Enter it.
Let your shoulders drop. Let your jaw soften. Let your hands open.
The world is too much with us; late and soon. Tonight, put it down.
To sleep is to trust. To trust is to be free.
Welcome the night as a friend who has known you a long time.
The night belongs to no one but the dreamer.
The day was long. You were enough.
Rest is sacred. Treat it as such.
Let the candle of thought burn down. Tomorrow brings its own match.
Drift, do not strain. The night does the work.
Lay down what you cannot carry through the night.
The wise rest themselves into their power.
To say goodnight is a kind of prayer.
Sleep is a sweet escape from what we could not solve today.
The night does not require your understanding, only your trust.
Be still, and let the world keep turning without you.
Lying down is a small act of faith in tomorrow.
Within the heart of every storm, there is stillness. Find it.
Even the longest day eventually puts itself to bed.
There is nothing the soul needs as much as rest.
Now is the only time. Tomorrow does not need you yet.
The body rests. The mind rests. The story can wait.
Sleep softly, you who carry quiet revolutions in your chest.
The stars do not compete. They simply shine.
Let the night undress you of the day.
There is great suffering in resisting what is. Let it be.
Let your breath out longer than you let it in. The body listens.
Stop, slow down, and the unhurried mind opens.
Trust the night. It has caught everyone who has ever lived.
The bed is an altar to tomorrow. Lie down with reverence.
Yesterday is gone. Tomorrow is not promised. Tonight is plenty.
The night is a quiet teacher.
Tonight is enough. You are enough.
Be where your feet are. They are in bed.
Stop holding the day. The day was never yours to hold.
Even the gods sleep.
Soft becomes everything that softens.
The night will not judge how much you got done.
The body forgives faster than the mind. Lie down and let it.
Sometimes the most productive thing you can do is rest.
Yesterday's leaves do not weigh down today's branch.
The day is done. You did not have to win it.
Wisdom begins in wonder. Wonder begins in stillness.
Soft pillow, soft mind.
May you wake gently. May you sleep deeply.
Do not bring the war of the day into the country of the night.
Each breath out is a small letting go. Keep going.
Lie down. The earth is holding you.
Be the still point in the turning of the world.
Tonight, the only task is the next breath.
Sleep finds those who stop searching.
The hour grows late. The heart grows soft.
The light goes out. The light within stays on.
The night is a great quiet hand.
A weary heart is welcome here.
Even Atlas sets down the world to sleep.
The thread of the day is tied. Let it go.
Soft is the night. Soften with it.
Hush, my mind. The day has had its turn.
Surrender is not weakness. Surrender is letting tomorrow be tomorrow.
Close the eyes. The world keeps spinning, kindly, without you.
Tonight, you owe nothing. Not even your worry.

wake.text: Discipline is the bridge between goals and accomplishment.
wake.author: Jim Rohn

wake.text: You will never always be motivated. You must learn to be disciplined.
wake.author: David Goggins

wake.text: Stay hard.
wake.author: David Goggins

wake.text: Don't count the days, make the days count.
wake.author: Muhammad Ali

wake.text: Champions are made from something deep inside them, not in gyms.
wake.author: Muhammad Ali

wake.text: Wake up determined, go to bed satisfied.
wake.author: George Lorimer

wake.text: Either you run the day, or the day runs you.
wake.author: Jim Rohn

wake.text: Don't wish it were easier. Wish you were better.
wake.author: Jim Rohn

wake.text: Suffer the pain of discipline or suffer the pain of regret.
wake.author: Jim Rohn

wake.text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
wake.author: Seneca

wake.text: While we wait for life, life passes.
wake.author: Seneca

wake.text: The man who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones.
wake.author: Confucius

wake.text: He who conquers himself is the mightiest warrior.
wake.author: Confucius

wake.text: Start where you are. Use what you have. Do what you can.
wake.author: Arthur Ashe

wake.text: You do not rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems.
wake.author: James Clear

wake.text: Habits are the compound interest of self-improvement.
wake.author: James Clear

wake.text: Confine yourself to the present.
wake.author: Marcus Aurelius

wake.text: At dawn, when you have trouble getting out of bed, tell yourself: I have to go to work, as a human being.
wake.author: Marcus Aurelius

wake.text: Waste no more time arguing what a good man should be. Be one.
wake.author: Marcus Aurelius

wake.text: We become what we repeatedly do.
wake.author: Will Durant

wake.text: Hard choices, easy life. Easy choices, hard life.
wake.author: Jerzy Gregorek

wake.text: If you are tired, learn to rest, not to quit.
wake.author: Banksy

wake.text: The successful warrior is the average man, with laser-like focus.
wake.author: Bruce Lee

wake.text: Do not pray for an easy life. Pray for the strength to endure a difficult one.
wake.author: Bruce Lee

wake.text: Whether you think you can or you think you can't, you're right.
wake.author: Henry Ford

wake.text: Discipline equals freedom.
wake.author: Jocko Willink

wake.text: Get after it.
wake.author: Jocko Willink

wake.text: There is no growth in comfort and no comfort in growth.
wake.author: Jocko Willink

wake.text: Action is the antidote to despair.
wake.author: Joan Baez

wake.text: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
wake.author: Mark Twain

wake.text: Don't be afraid to fail. Be afraid not to try.
wake.author: Michael Jordan

wake.text: I've failed over and over and over again in my life. And that is why I succeed.
wake.author: Michael Jordan

wake.text: A goal without a plan is just a wish.
wake.author: Antoine de Saint-Exupery

wake.text: The cave you fear to enter holds the treasure you seek.
wake.author: Joseph Campbell

wake.text: Hard work beats talent when talent doesn't work hard.
wake.author: Tim Notke

wake.text: Run when you can, walk if you have to, crawl if you must; just never give up.
wake.author: Dean Karnazes

wake.text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
wake.author: Friedrich Nietzsche

wake.text: He who would learn to fly must first learn to stand and walk and run.
wake.author: Friedrich Nietzsche

wake.text: You are what you do, not what you say you'll do.
wake.author: Carl Jung

wake.text: Discipline yourself, and others won't have to.
wake.author: John Wooden

wake.text: To begin, begin.
wake.author: William Wordsworth

wake.text: Excellence is not an act, but a habit.
wake.author: Aristotle

wake.text: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is a habit.
wake.author: Aristotle

wake.text: Doubt kills more dreams than failure ever will.
wake.author: Suzy Kassem

wake.text: You have exactly the same number of hours as Leonardo da Vinci.
wake.author: H. Jackson Brown Jr.

wake.text: If it is important to you, you will find a way. If not, you will find an excuse.
wake.author: Ryan Blair

wake.text: Comfort is the enemy of progress.
wake.author: P.T. Barnum

wake.text: Don't watch the clock; do what it does. Keep going.
wake.author: Sam Levenson

wake.text: If we wait for the moment when everything is ready, we shall never begin.
wake.author: Ivan Turgenev

wake.text: The price of greatness is responsibility.
wake.author: Winston Churchill

wake.text: Don't let yesterday take up too much of today.
wake.author: Will Rogers

wake.text: There is no elevator to success. You have to take the stairs.
wake.author: Zig Ziglar

wake.text: He who is not busy being born is busy dying.
wake.author: Bob Dylan

wake.text: The brave man is not he who does not feel afraid, but he who conquers that fear.
wake.author: Nelson Mandela

wake.text: It always seems impossible until it's done.
wake.author: Nelson Mandela

wake.text: Pain plus reflection equals progress.
wake.author: Ray Dalio

wake.text: Do or do not. There is no try.
wake.author: Yoda

wake.text: Whatever you are, be a good one.
wake.author: Abraham Lincoln

wake.text: The first hour is the rudder of the day.
wake.author: Henry Ward Beecher

wake.text: A man is what he pays attention to.
wake.author: Naval Ravikant

wake.text: Believe you can and you're halfway there.
wake.author: Theodore Roosevelt

wake.text: Do what you can, with what you have, where you are.
wake.author: Theodore Roosevelt

wake.text: I have not failed. I've found 10,000 ways that won't work.
wake.author: Thomas Edison

wake.text: You miss 100% of the shots you don't take.
wake.author: Wayne Gretzky

wake.text: The future depends on what you do today.
wake.author: Mahatma Gandhi

wake.text: The way to get started is to quit talking and begin doing.
wake.author: Walt Disney

wake.text: If you can dream it, you can do it.
wake.author: Walt Disney

wake.text: Always do what you are afraid to do.
wake.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

wake.text: Out of difficulties grow miracles.
wake.author: Jean de La Bruyere

wake.text: Opportunities don't happen. You create them.
wake.author: Chris Grosser

wake.text: Success usually comes to those who are too busy to be looking for it.
wake.author: Henry David Thoreau

wake.text: The man on top of the mountain didn't fall there.
wake.author: Vince Lombardi

wake.text: Nothing will work unless you do.
wake.author: Maya Angelou

wake.text: Setting goals is the first step in turning the invisible into the visible.
wake.author: Tony Robbins

wake.text: Either I will find a way, or I will make one.
wake.author: Hannibal

wake.text: I am not a product of my circumstances. I am a product of my decisions.
wake.author: Stephen Covey

wake.text: Don't wait. The time will never be just right.
wake.author: Napoleon Hill

wake.text: When you arise in the morning, give thanks for the morning light.
wake.author: Tecumseh

wake.text: Sometimes you have to do what you don't like to get where you want to be.
wake.author: Stephen A. Smith

wake.text: Show up. Even on the bad days. Especially on the bad days.
Get up. Today is not optional.
You can't be lazy and great at the same time.
The day is yours. Take it.
Bed is a trap. The world is the prize.
Don't be the architect of your own decay. Get up.
You don't need more motivation. You need to move.
Pain is temporary. Sleeping past noon is permanent.
The world doesn't owe you tomorrow. It owes you a morning to take it.
The morning is when the gods watch what you do first.
You will never have this morning again. Spend it well.
The clock will not give you time back.
Wake up. The version of you who got everything done today is waiting.
The grind doesn't care that you're tired. Neither should you.
The cost of inaction is far greater than the cost of failure.
The first step is always the hardest. And the most important.
Move with the urgency of a man on fire.
Begin. The rest is easier.
The world is yours, but only if you get up to claim it.
Sunlight is medicine. So is movement. Take both at dawn.
There is no traffic jam on the extra mile.
Be a warrior, not a worrier.
Energy follows attention. Attention follows breath. Breath follows getting up.
Lazy is a learned behavior. Unlearn it today.
Don't trade what you want most for what you want right now.
Do something today that your future self will thank you for.
The morning belongs to those who claim it.
You did not come this far to only come this far.
Stand up. The world is asking for you.
The man who wakes early outpaces the man who plans to.
Mediocrity tastes the same every day. Greatness tastes different.
There is no hack. There is only get up.
Your future is built between the alarm and your feet on the floor.
The world rewards motion. Even imperfect motion.
You are not tired. You are untrained.
Build the habit of doing what you said you would.
You cannot scroll your way to greatness.
There is no later. There is only now and never.
Light comes to those who rise.
Difficulty is a coin the wise spend gladly.
Some people dream of success. Others wake up and work for it.
Coffee, water, sunlight, motion. The day responds to ritual.
Get up. Make the bed. The day starts to listen.
Show up sweaty. Show up nervous. Just show up.
There is no shortcut. The shortcut is doing the long thing daily.
You are one decision away from a totally different life.
Strength is the product of struggle. Wake up and struggle.
Be brave enough to suck at something new. Today is a good day.
Be stronger than your strongest excuse.
Wake up and live.
Conquer yourself, and the day is conquered.
Today is the youngest you will ever be. Act like it.
The early hour rewards everything.
Move first. Think second. Doubt never.
You do not need permission to begin.
The sun is up. The world is open. Walk in.
The future is hidden in your morning routine.
Carry yourself like the person you want to become.
Mornings are not for excuses; they are for ignition.
Either you control the morning, or the morning controls you.
Conquer the morning. Inherit the day.
The body warms once it moves. So does the mind. So does the will.
The pain you feel today will be the strength you feel tomorrow.
Hard now, soft later. Soft now, hard later. Choose.
An alarm is a small contract with the person you want to become.
Discomfort is the currency of growth. Spend it before noon.

windDown.text: Walk as if you are kissing the Earth with your feet.
windDown.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

windDown.text: Drink your tea slowly and reverently.
windDown.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

windDown.text: When you walk, arrive with every step.
windDown.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

windDown.text: The first wealth is health.
windDown.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

windDown.text: Sleep is good, and books are better.
windDown.author: George R.R. Martin

windDown.text: Little by little, one travels far.
windDown.author: J.R.R. Tolkien

windDown.text: Tomorrow is fresh, with no mistakes in it yet.
windDown.author: L.M. Montgomery

windDown.text: How we spend our days is, of course, how we spend our lives.
windDown.author: Annie Dillard

windDown.text: Pay attention. Be astonished. Tell about it.
windDown.author: Mary Oliver

windDown.text: Listen, are you breathing just a little, and calling it a life?
windDown.author: Mary Oliver

windDown.text: Just for today, do not worry. Just for today, do not anger.
windDown.author: Mikao Usui

windDown.text: Whatever the present moment contains, accept it as if you had chosen it.
windDown.author: Eckhart Tolle

windDown.text: Health is the greatest gift; contentment the greatest wealth.
windDown.author: Buddha

windDown.text: Drop by drop is the water pot filled.
windDown.author: Buddha

windDown.text: Calmness is the cradle of power.
windDown.author: J.G. Holland

windDown.text: Quiet minds cannot be perplexed or frightened.
windDown.author: Robert Louis Stevenson

windDown.text: Take care of your body. It's the only place you have to live.
windDown.author: Jim Rohn

windDown.text: Solitude is the soul's holiday.
windDown.author: Katrina Kenison

windDown.text: The world is full of magic things, patiently waiting for our senses to grow sharper.
windDown.author: W. B. Yeats

windDown.text: Each moment is a place you've never been.
windDown.author: Mark Strand

windDown.text: Tomorrow is another day.
windDown.author: Margaret Mitchell

windDown.text: Look deep into nature, and you will understand everything better.
windDown.author: Albert Einstein

windDown.text: Slow and steady wins the race.
windDown.author: Aesop

windDown.text: Routine is liberty.
windDown.author: Mason Cooley

windDown.text: There is no day so bad that it can't be fixed with a nap.
windDown.author: Carrie Snow

windDown.text: Walking is man's best medicine.
windDown.author: Hippocrates

windDown.text: Let food be thy medicine and medicine be thy food.
windDown.author: Hippocrates

windDown.text: Silence is a true friend who never betrays.
windDown.author: Confucius

windDown.text: Yesterday is gone. We have only today. Let us begin.
windDown.author: Mother Teresa

windDown.text: Care of the soul is a deep and full appreciation of what is now.
windDown.author: Thomas Moore

windDown.text: Early to bed and early to rise makes a man healthy, wealthy, and wise.
windDown.author: Benjamin Franklin

windDown.text: An hour's sleep before midnight is worth two after.
windDown.author: English proverb

windDown.text: Time you enjoy wasting is not wasted time.
windDown.author: Marthe Troly-Curtin

windDown.text: Be like the bamboo: the higher you grow, the deeper you bow.
windDown.author: Chinese proverb

windDown.text: He who has health has hope; he who has hope has everything.
windDown.author: Arabian proverb

windDown.text: Routine, in an intelligent man, is a sign of ambition.
windDown.author: W. H. Auden

windDown.text: Self-care is how you take your power back.
windDown.author: Lalah Delia

windDown.text: The body benefits from movement, and the mind benefits from stillness.
windDown.author: Sakyong Mipham

windDown.text: If you light a lamp for somebody, it will also brighten your path.
windDown.author: Buddha

windDown.text: Mindfulness is the aware, balanced acceptance of the present experience.
windDown.author: Sylvia Boorstein

windDown.text: Order is the shape upon which beauty depends.
windDown.author: Pearl S. Buck

windDown.text: If you are losing your leisure, look out; you may be losing your soul.
windDown.author: Logan Pearsall Smith

windDown.text: Sleep, riches, and health, to be truly enjoyed, must be interrupted.
windDown.author: Jean Paul Richter

windDown.text: Each day is a little life; every waking is a little birth.
windDown.author: Arthur Schopenhauer

windDown.text: Dim the lights. Begin to slow.
Put the phone down. Pick yourself up.
The day is ending. So is its claim on you.
Light a candle. The mood follows the room.
Breathe in for four. Out for six. Sleep is close.
Stretch your shoulders. Soften your jaw. Slow your pace.
Make tea, not plans.
The notification can wait. Sleep cannot.
Lower the screen. Raise the heart.
Three slow breaths. The body recognizes the cue.
Close the tabs. Close the day.
Gratitude before sleep is the gentlest prayer.
Name three good things from today. Then let the rest go.
The next hour is for softness. Treat it kindly.
Walk the room slowly. Let the body remember it is tired.
Wash your face. Wash the day away.
Lower the temperature. Lower the volume. Lower the pace.
Drink water now so you do not wake up needing it.
Read fiction, not feeds.
Lay tomorrow's clothes out. Lay tomorrow's worries down.
Make the bed inviting. Make the room inviting. Make sleep easy.
Put your phone in another room. You will sleep deeper.
Tonight's wind-down is tomorrow's energy.
Light is a drug. Use less of it now.
Slow conversations only. Slow music only. Slow thoughts only.
Soft socks. Soft sheets. Soft mind.
Choose stillness over scrolling.
Cool the room. The body wants cool air, not warm.
The day's last task: be kind to yourself.
Don't pack tomorrow into tonight.
Read a few pages. Pages put the mind to bed.
A warm shower tells the body it is time.
Brush your teeth. Brush off the day.
Lights low. Voice low. Mind low.
A short journal entry is a long, deep sleep.
Speak softly tonight, even to yourself.
Begin the descent. The runway is the bed.
End the day with the same care you began it.
Pour something warm. Sit somewhere quiet.
Inhale: thank you. Exhale: goodnight.
Curtains closed. Notifications off. Day closed.
The room becomes a cave. The cave becomes a cradle.
Tonight, do one less thing. It is enough.
The last hour of the day is sacred. Defend it.
Stretch the spine. Quiet the spine. Rest the spine.
Yawn deliberately. The body learns to yawn back.
A single deep breath can change the next eight hours.
Set your alarm. Then forget about morning.
Whisper a small thank you to the day.
Cool sheets. Quiet room. Slow breath.
Wind-down is not lazy; it is craft.
